_meta.description: Rotating angle seeds for X text post generation. Insight, identity-scene, and story-seed entries — refreshed weekly by intelligence engine. Injected into generate-x-posts.mjs as inspiration (never verbatim copy).

topics.text: Most kids solve a maze faster on the second attempt than any adult on the first.
Screen-free activities don't replace screens — they train the attention span that makes screens less necessary.
The hardest puzzle for a 5-year-old is the one they're two steps away from finishing — that gap is where persistence lives.
Fine motor skills are the one thing pediatric OTs keep asking about — and the one thing most screen time skips entirely.
You're the parent who paused the show because you heard them say 'wait, I almost have it.'
You printed it out just to see what they'd do with it. Three hours later you still haven't cleaned the table.
You're the one who bought crayons 'for them' and keeps finding yourself picking the colors first.
The Saturday morning where nothing was planned and everything went exactly right.
The crayon on the floor. The finished page on the fridge. The hour you didn't notice passing.
She asked me to turn the music off so she could concentrate on her coloring.
